title: caltha biflora var. rotundifolia
intro: Caltha biflora var. rotundifolia, also known as the Marsh Marigold, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The medicinal properties of Caltha biflora var. rotundifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides in the plant. The health benefits of Caltha biflora var. rotundifolia include its use as a treatment for skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Caltha biflora var. rotundifolia can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
